q: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is ______.
question: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is ______.
solution:
log4x-1=log2x-3

⇒12log2x-1=log2x-3

⇒log2x-11/2=log2x-3

⇒x-11/2=x-3

⇒x-1=x2+9-6x

⇒x2-7x+10=0

⇒x-2x-5=0

⇒x=2,5

But x≠2 because it is not satisfying the domain of given equation i.e log2x-3→ its domain x>3 finally x is 5

∴ No. of solutions =1.

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mi> </mi><mo>:</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mi> </mi><mo>&</mo><mi> </mi><mn>2</mn><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> . subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of squares of all the real solutions of the equation $\log_{(x+1)}(2x^2+5x+3) = 4 - \log_{(2x+3)}(x^2+2x+1)$ is equal to ________.
question: The sum of squares of all the real solutions of the equation $\log_{(x+1)}(2x^2+5x+3) = 4 - \log_{(2x+3)}(x^2+2x+1)$ is equal to ________.
solution:
For the logarithms to be defined, we must satisfy the following conditions:
1. Base of the first logarithm: $x+1 > 0 \Rightarrow x > -1$ and $x+1 eq 1 \Rightarrow x eq 0$.
2. Base of the second logarithm: $2x+3 > 0 \Rightarrow x > -\dfrac{3}{2}$ and $2x+3 eq 1 \Rightarrow x eq -1$.
3. Arguments must be positive: $2x^2+5x+3 > 0$ and $x^2+2x+1 > 0$.
Taking the intersection of all these conditions, the domain of the equation is $x \in (-1, 0) \cup (0, \infty)$.

Now, factorizing the arguments of the logarithms:
$2x^2+5x+3 = (2x+3)(x+1)$
$x^2+2x+1 = (x+1)^2$

Substitute these into the given equation:
$\log_{(x+1)}((2x+3)(x+1)) = 4 - \log_{(2x+3)}((x+1)^2)$

Using the properties of logarithms:
$\log_{(x+1)}(2x+3) + \log_{(x+1)}(x+1) = 4 - 2\log_{(2x+3)}(x+1)$
$\log_{(x+1)}(2x+3) + 1 = 4 - \dfrac{2}{\log_{(x+1)}(2x+3)}$

Let $t = \log_{(x+1)}(2x+3)$. The equation becomes:
$t + 1 = 4 - \dfrac{2}{t}$
$t - 3 + \dfrac{2}{t} = 0$
$t^2 - 3t + 2 = 0$
$(t-1)(t-2) = 0$
$\Rightarrow t = 1$ or $t = 2$

Case 1: $t = 1$
$\log_{(x+1)}(2x+3) = 1$
$2x+3 = x+1$
$x = -2$
This value is rejected because $x = -2$ does not fall in the domain $x > -1$.

Case 2: $t = 2$
$\log_{(x+1)}(2x+3) = 2$
$2x+3 = (x+1)^2$
$2x+3 = x^2+2x+1$
$x^2 = 2$
$x = \pm\sqrt{2}$

Since $x > -1$, $x = -\sqrt{2}$ is rejected. The only valid solution is $x = \sqrt{2}$.

The sum of squares of all the real solutions is $(\sqrt{2})^2 = 2$.

Answer: $2$

subject: Mathematics
q: The product of all solutions of the equation $\mathrm{e}^{5\left(\log _{\mathrm{e}} x\right)^2+3}=x^8, x\gt0$, is :
question: The product of all solutions of the equation $\mathrm{e}^{5\left(\log _{\mathrm{e}} x\right)^2+3}=x^8, x\gt0$, is :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{e}^{5(\ln x)^2+3}=\mathrm{x}^8 \\ & \Rightarrow \ell \mathrm{ne}^{5(\ln x)^2+3}=\ell \mathrm{nn}^8 \\ & \Rightarrow 5(\ln \mathrm{x})^2+3=8 \ell \mathrm{nx} \\ & (\ell \mathrm{nx}=\mathrm{t}) \\ & \Rightarrow 5 \mathrm{t}^2-8 \mathrm{t}+3=0 \\ & \quad \mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2=\frac{8}{5} \\ & \quad \ln \mathrm{x}_1 \mathrm{x}_2=\frac{8}{5} \\ & \mathrm{x}_1 \mathrm{X}_2=\mathrm{e}^{8 / 5}\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of the real solutions of the equation: $x|x+3|+|x-1|-2=0$ is
question: The number of the real solutions of the equation: $x|x+3|+|x-1|-2=0$ is
solution:
Critical points: $x=-3$ and $x=1$.
Case $x < -3$:
$x^2+4x+1=0 \Rightarrow x=-2\pm\sqrt{3}$.
Only $x=-2-\sqrt{3} \approx -3.73$ lies in $(-\infty,-3)$. (1 solution)
Case $-3 \le x < 1$:
$x^2+2x-1=0 \Rightarrow x=-1\pm\sqrt{2}$.
Both $x=-1+\sqrt{2} \approx 0.41$ and $x=-1-\sqrt{2} \approx -2.41$ lie in $[-3,1)$. (2 solutions)
Case $x \ge 1$:
$x^2+4x-3=0 \Rightarrow x=-2\pm\sqrt{7}$.
Neither root $\ge 1$. (0 solutions)
Total number of real solutions $= 3$.

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mfrac><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>Z</mi><mo>:</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>≤</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>T</mi></math> is
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mfrac><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>Z</mi><mo>:</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>≤</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>T</mi></math> is
solution:
Given, x2-7x+9≤0

⇒x≤7±132

⇒x∈7-132,7+132

Also given x∈Integers, so x can be ±2, ±3, ±4, ±5

Now out of these values of x only 3, -4, -5 will satisfy S=x+3-1x-2≥0

So, S∩T∈ 3, -4, -5

So, nS∩T=3

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of the roots of the equation, x+1-2log23+2x+2log410-2-x=0, is :
question: The sum of the roots of the equation, x+1-2log23+2x+2log410-2-x=0, is :
solution:
Given, x+1-2log23+2x+2log410-2-x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+2log2210.2x-12x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+log210.2x-12x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+log210.2x-1-log22x=0

⇒x+1-log23+2x2+log210.2x-1-xlog22=0

⇒x+1-log23+2x2+log210.2x-1-x=0

⇒x+1+log210.2x-13+2x2-x=0

⇒1+log210.2x-13+2x2=0

⇒log210.2x-13+2x2=-1

⇒log210.2x-19+2x2+6.2x=log212

⇒10.2x-19+2x2+6.2x=12

⇒210.2x-1=9+2x2+6.2x

⇒2x2-14.2x+11=0

Let 2x=y

⇒y2-14y+11=0

Let roots are y1=2x1 & y2=2x2

Product of roots, y1y2=2x1×2x2=2x1+x2=11

⇒log2x1+x2=log11

⇒x1+x2log2=log11

⇒x1+x2=log11log2

Hence, x1+x2=log211

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> is
question: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> is
solution:
We have,

log(x+1)2x2+7x+5+log(2x+5)(x+1)2-4=0, x>0

⇒logx+12x+5x+1+2log2x+1x+1=4

⇒logx+12x+5+logx+1x+1+2log2x+1x+1=4

⇒logx+12x+5+1+2log2x+1x+1=4

⇒logx+12x+5+2log2x+1x+1=3

⇒logx+12x+5+2logx+12x+5=3

Put log(x+1)(2x+5)=t, then

t+2t=3

⇒t2-3t+2=0

⇒t=1, 2

Then,

log(x+1)(2x+5)=1 and log(x+1)(2x+5)=2

⇒2x+5=x+1 and 2x+5=x+12

x=-4 (rejected) as x>0

And,

2x+5=x+12

⇒x2=4

 ⇒x=2,-2 (rejected)

So, x=2

Number of solution is 1.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the solution of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>β</mi></msqrt></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are integers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If the solution of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>β</mi></msqrt></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are integers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given:

logcosxcotx+4logsinxtanx=1, x∈0,π2

⇒lncosx-lnsinxlncosx+4lnsinx-lncosxlnsinx=1

⇒1-lnsinxlncosx+41-lncosxlnsinx=1

⇒lnsinxlncosx+4lncosxlnsinx-4=0

⇒(lnsinx)2-4(lnsinx)(lncosx)+4(lncosx)2=0

⇒lnsinx-2lncosx2=0

⇒lnsinx=2lncosx

⇒lnsinx=lncos2x

⇒cos2x=sinx

⇒1-sin2x=sinx

⇒sin2x+sinx-1=0

⇒sinx=-1+52

So, α=-1, β=5

∴α+β=4

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of integral solution <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>7</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></msub><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is
question: The number of integral solution <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>log</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>7</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></msub><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is
solution:
We have been given thatlogx+72x-72x-32≥0

We need to find the domain.

For loggxfx is defined when fx>0 and gx>0, gx≠1.

⇒x+72>0

⇒x>-72

Also, x+72≠1

⇒x≠-52

And x-72x-3≠0

⇒x≠7 and x≠32

Domain : -72,∞--52,0,32

Now let us take different cases.

Case I: 0<x+72<1
⇒-72<x<-52.....(1)

⇒x-72x-32≤1

⇒-1≤x-72x-3≤1

⇒x-72x-3+1≥0

⇒x-7+2x-32x-3≥0

⇒3x-102x-3≥0...(2)

And x-72x-3-1≤0

⇒x-7-2x+32x-3≤0

⇒-x-42x-3≤0

⇒x+42x-3≥0....(3)

From 1,2,3 we can tell that there is no intersection, no solution

Case II:

⇒x+72>1

⇒x>-52

⇒x-72x-32≥1

⇒x-72x-3≥1

⇒x-72x-3≤-1

⇒x∈32,103

Or,x-72x-3≥1

⇒x-7-2x+32x-3≥0

⇒x+42x-3≤0

⇒x∈-4,32

x∈-52,32∪32,103

Hence there are total 6 integers -2,-1,0,1,2,3.

Hence this is the required option.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi></math> be the three distinct positive real numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>a</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>b</mi></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>b</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>c</mi></mrow></msup></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi></math> is equal to ______.
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi></math> be the three distinct positive real numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>a</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>b</mi></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>b</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>c</mi></mrow></msup></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi></math> is equal to ______.
solution:
Given equation: 2alna=bclnb

⇒lna·ln2a=lnb·lnbc

⇒lna·ln2+lna=lnb·lnb+lnc

let lna=x, lnb=y, lnc=z, x≠y≠z

⇒xln2+x=yy+z

⇒xln2=y2-x2+yz ........(i)

Similarly, from second equation

ln2·lnb=lnc·lna

⇒ln2=xzy ......(ii)

Substitute eqii in eqi,

We get,

⇒y3-yx2+y2z=x2z

⇒y2y+z-x2y+z=0

⇒y2-x2y+z=0

⇒x-yx+yy+z=0

∵ x≠y⇒x+yy+z=0

Now x=-y, y=-z and x=z

But ln2=xzy

⇒ln2=xx-x

⇒ln2=-lna

=-ln 2⇒a=12 and

⇒y=-z

⇒lnb=-lnc

⇒bc=1

Now 6a+5bc=612+51=8

Hence this is the required answer.

subject: Mathematics
q: If for x∈0,π2,log10sinx+log10cosx=-1 and log10sinx+cosx=12log10n-1,n>0, then the value of n is equal to :
question: If for x∈0,π2,log10sinx+log10cosx=-1 and log10sinx+cosx=12log10n-1,n>0, then the value of n is equal to :
solution:
Given x∈0,π2

log10sinx+log10cosx=-1

⇒log10sinx.cosx=-1

⇒sinx.cosx=110   ...(1)

log10sinx+cosx=12log10n-1

⇒sinx+cosx=10log10n-12=10log10n-log1010=n10

By squaring we get, 

sin2x+cos2x+2sinxcosx=n10

1+2sinx.cosx=n10

⇒1+15=n10⇒n=12

subject: Mathematics
q: If the set of all solutions of $|x^2 + x - 9| = |x| + |x^2 - 9|$ is $[\alpha, \beta] \cup [\gamma, \infty)$, then $(\alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2)$ is equal to:
question: If the set of all solutions of $|x^2 + x - 9| = |x| + |x^2 - 9|$ is $[\alpha, \beta] \cup [\gamma, \infty)$, then $(\alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2)$ is equal to:
solution:
The given equation is $|x^2 + x - 9| = |x| + |x^2 - 9|$.

Let $A = x$ and $B = x^2 - 9$. Then $A + B = x^2 + x - 9$.

The equation is of the form $|A + B| = |A| + |B|$, which holds true if and only if $A \cdot B \ge 0$.

Substituting the values of $A$ and $B$:

$x(x^2 - 9) \ge 0$

$x(x - 3)(x + 3) \ge 0$

The critical points are $-3, 0, 3$. Using the wavy curve method, the solution set is:

$x \in [-3, 0] \cup [3, \infty)$

Comparing this with the given solution set $[\alpha, \beta] \cup [\gamma, \infty)$, we get:

$\alpha = -3$, $\beta = 0$, $\gamma = 3$

We need to find the value of $\alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2$:

$\alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2 = (-3)^2 + 0^2 + 3^2 = 9 + 0 + 9 = 18$

Answer: $18$

q: The number of distinct solutions of the equation, <math><mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">log</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msub></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></mrow></mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">log</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msub></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></math> in the interval <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> is ________
question: The number of distinct solutions of the equation, <math><mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">log</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msub></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></mrow></mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">log</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msub></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></math> in the interval <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> is ________
solution:
log1/2⁡sin⁡x=2-log1/2⁡cos⁡x
log1/2sin⁡xcos⁡x=2
sin⁡xcos⁡x=14
sin⁡2x=±12

Number of solution =8 .

subject: Mathematics
q: The least integral value $\alpha$ of $x$ such that $\frac{x-5}{x^2+5 x-14}>0$, satisfies :
question: The least integral value $\alpha$ of $x$ such that $\frac{x-5}{x^2+5 x-14}>0$, satisfies :
solution:
$$
\text { } \begin{aligned}
& \frac{x-5}{x^2+5 x-14}>0 \Rightarrow x^2+5 x-14 < x-5 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+4 x-9 < 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \alpha=-5,-4,-3,-2,-1,0,1
\end{aligned}
$$
$\alpha=-5$ does not satisfy any of the options
$\alpha=-4$ satisfy the option (a) $\alpha^2+3 \alpha-4=0$

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>{</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn><mo>}</mo><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>{</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>⩾</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>}</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> is the set of all integers, then the number of subsets of the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>C</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi>c</mi></msup><mo>∩</mo><mi>Z</mi></math> is _________.
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>{</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn><mo>}</mo><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>{</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>⩾</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>}</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> is the set of all integers, then the number of subsets of the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>C</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi>c</mi></msup><mo>∩</mo><mi>Z</mi></math> is _________.
solution:
For First set A={x∈R:|x-2|>1}

⇒ Value which A contains lies from x∈-∞,1∪3,∞

For second set B=x∈R:x2-3>1

⇒ Value which B contains lies from x∈-∞,-2∪2,∞

For third set C={x∈R:|x-4|⩾2}

⇒ Value which C contains lies from x∈(-∞,2]∪[6,∞)

Now, -∞,-2∪[6,∞)∈A∩B∩C

Therefore, A∩B∩Cc∩Z=-2,-1,0,1,2,3,4,5

Number of Subsets of above set =28=256

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>≥</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then which one the following statements is NOT true?
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>≥</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then which one the following statements is NOT true?
solution:
Given,

A=x∈R:x+1<2

⇒A=-3,1

Now, B=x∈R:x-1≥2

⇒B=(-∞,-1]∪[3,∞)

Now, B-A=(-∞,-3]∪[3,∞)

⇒B-A=R--3,3

So, option B is not true.

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>≥</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>;</mo></math> then
solution:
A=x:x∈-2,2

B=x:x∈(-∞,-1]∪[5,∞

A∩B=x:x∈(-2,-1]

A∪B=x:x∈-∞,2∪5,∞)

A-B=x:x∈-1,2

B-A=x:x∈-∞,-2∪5,∞

subject: Mathematics
q:
Statement - 1: For every natural number $n \geq 2, \frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}>\sqrt{n}$.
Statement $-2$ : For every natural number $n \geq 2, \sqrt{n(n+1)} < n+1$.
question:
Statement - 1: For every natural number $n \geq 2, \frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}>\sqrt{n}$.
Statement $-2$ : For every natural number $n \geq 2, \sqrt{n(n+1)} < n+1$.
options: Statement $-1$ is false, Statement $-2$ is true
Statement $-1$ is true, Statement $-2$ is true, Statement $-2$ is a correct explanation for Statement $-1$
Statement $-1$ is true, Statement $-2$ is true; Statement $-2$ is not a correct explanation for Statement $-1$.
Statement $-1$ is true, Statement $-2$ is false.
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P(n)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \\
& P(2)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}>\sqrt{2}
\end{aligned}
$$
Let us assume that $P(k)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{k}}>\sqrt{k}$ is true $\therefore P(k+1)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{k}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{k+1}}>\sqrt{k+1}$ has to be true. L.H.S. $>\sqrt{\mathrm{k}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}+1}}=\frac{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{k}+1)}+1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}+1}}$
Since $\sqrt{k(k+1)}>k \quad(\forall k \geq 0)$
$$
\therefore \frac{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{k}+1)}+1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}+1}}>\frac{\mathrm{k}+1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{k}+1}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{k}+1}
$$
Let $P(n)=\sqrt{n(n+1)} < n+1$
Statement $-1$ is correct.
$$
P(2)=\sqrt{2 \times 3} < 3
$$
If $P(k)=\sqrt{k(k+1)} < (k+1)$ is true
Now $P(k+1)=\sqrt{(k+1)(k+2)} < k+2$ has to be true
Since $(k+1) < k+2$
$$
\therefore \sqrt{(\mathrm{k}+1)(\mathrm{k}+2)} < (\mathrm{k}+2)
$$
Hence Statement $-2$ is not a correct explanation of Statement $-1$.
